title: Nobel Laureate Wole Soyinka's Visa Revoked
description_html: The Department of State revoked the B1/B2 visa of Nigerian Nobel laureate Wole Soyinka, who said he had been informed by the U.S. Consulate on October 23 that “additional information” had emerged, prompting the cancellation of his visa under 22 CFR § 41.122. The U.S. Embassy in Lagos, Nigeria, stated that visas are privileges, not rights, and can be revoked at any time under U.S. law, while declining to discuss individual cases for confidentiality reasons.
